name: EQ Parametric
description: This algorithm is a multi-channel, multi-band, parametric EQ. Each channel can process a number of busses - you might typically have a channel processing one or two busses for a mono or stereo signal, but you could, say, process all 12 of the inputs with the same EQ settings by using a single channel with a width of 12.
gui_description: The display shows a graphical representation of the EQ curve for the current channel. The frequency and gain of the current band within the channel is indicated with a small box.

parameters.name: Channels
parameters.description: The number of bus channels to process.

parameters.name: Bands
parameters.description: The number of EQ bands per channel.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The number of busses to process, starting from the Input. For example, for a stereo signal, set this to 2.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Chooses the type of the EQ band. The options are 'Low pass (1st)', 'High pass (1st)', 'Low pass (2nd)', 'High pass (2nd)', 'Low shelf', 'High shelf', and 'Peak'.

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.description: Sets the EQ band centre frequency.

parameters.name: Q
parameters.description: Sets the EQ band 'Q' or resonance. Note that some EQ types do not use this parameter.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: Sets the EQ band gain. Note that some EQ types do not use this parameter.

input_ports.name: Input
input_ports.description: The first input bus to process.

output_ports.name: Output
output_ports.description: The first output bus. If set to 'None', the input bus is used as output, and the mode is always 'Replace'.
